calendar.matters: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jun-09-2026 Off Calendar
TENTATIVE RULING: Off calendar
EXAMINER: AERO FETH

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Continued To Jun-09-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: James O'Neil appeared via zoom for Timothy Curly (successor administrator).

Mr. O'Neil stated they are in the process of locating the mother and additional heirs that may be residing in Italy. The matter is continued to June 9, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Continued To Dec-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
1) PES-96-266566, Estate of Piero Castagnoli; and
2) PES-23-306310, Estate of Elizabeth Mary Castagnoli.

Appearances:
James O'Neil for administrator Timothy Kerley (via Zoom).

The matters came on for hearing on status of close of estates.

Mr. O'Neil reported that an attorney in Rome was retained to locate heirs of Piero Castagnoli believed to have an intestate interest. Records had been located in Milan, but confirmation of whether Piero's mother is living, and identification of heirs, remains outstanding. Mr. O'Neil explained that progress has been delayed by the slow pace of Italian bureaucracy.

Mr. O'Neil further explained that resolution in Piero's estate is necessary to determine distributions in Elizabeth's estate. The primary issue is whether one-half or the entirety of a house in San Francisco should flow to Elizabeth's estate. Mr. O'Neil estimated at least 90 additional days were needed, with a further three months to complete administration of Elizabeth's estate once information is received.

Following discussion, the Court continued the Piero Castagnoli matter to December 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Elizabeth Mary Castagnoli matter was continued to March 10, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Off Calendar

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-24-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James O' Neil for Timothy Kerley, Successor Administrator (via Zoom).

The Court held a hearing on the petition for approval of the report on the first status of administration. Mr. O'Neill acknowledged the tentative ruling and addressed outstanding issues, including lack of service on the surety and prior attorney, and reported that notice would be provided moving forward.

Mr. O'Neill explained that a significant issue in the administration involves efforts to determine the interest of the decedent's mother, who has not been located. Counsel in Italy has been retained to investigate whether she is deceased and to identify any heirs. Mr. O'Neill reported that they are awaiting documents such as death certificates and possible wills from Italian local authorities, but that progress has been slow due to the nature of the bureaucracy. An update is expected by the end of July.

The Court took the petition under submission with the intent to grant and set a hearing on status of close of estate for September 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar May-14-2025 Off Calendar

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-18-2025 Continued To Jun-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James O'Neil for Timothy Kerley, Successor Administrator (via Zoom).

Mr. O'Neil stated that notice had not been given to the bonding company or the former attorney. The Court instructed counsel to provide notice to the bonding company and the former attorney.

The court inquired as to the status of the Inventory and Appraisal. Mr. O'Neil stated that the signed I&A had been mailed to the probate referee but was lost in the mail. A new copy has been requested so that it can be re-sent to the probate referee. The court set a status hearing on filing the I&A for May 14, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The court continued the petition to June 17, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
